About
Request a Demo

The Visibility Layer

Monitor the assets you can't reach.

30–40% of critical rotating assets have no condition monitoring. The visibility layer closes that gap from the motor control cabinet.

Request a Demo

Three barriers. One structural problem.

Physical

The asset is underwater, sealed inside a machine, or buried in a location no technician can reach during operation. Vibration sensors need line-of-sight to the bearing housing. These assets don't allow it.

Economic

A vibration sensor costs €500. Installed on a submerged pump or inside an ATEX zone, it costs €5,000 or more. For the hundreds of auxiliary motors across a large site, the economics never close.

Regulatory

ATEX certification, confined space permits, and permit-to-work requirements turn a 30-minute sensor installation into a multi-day, multi-discipline project. Most sites don't bother.

The result: the assets most likely to fail are the ones with no data.

Read: The proximity penalty →

Your digitalisation investment stops at assets with no data.

Every plant has invested in systems that depend on asset-level data. SCADA collects it. CMMS acts on it. Digital twins model it. Predictive maintenance programmes are built on it.

These systems need three types of data from every rotating asset: condition (is it degrading?), performance (is it running efficiently?), and energy (how much power is it consuming?). For assets with vibration sensors or VFDs, some of this data exists. For the rest, none of it does.

The gap is largest where it matters most: brownfield plants with DOL motors.

A direct-on-line motor connected to a contactor has no digital interface. No drive capturing speed or torque. No sensor reading vibration. No efficiency data. No condition data. Nothing. It runs until it fails. In most brownfield plants, these motors are the majority of the installed base. They're the pumps, fans, and compressors commissioned 15 or 30 years ago, before anyone planned for digitalisation.

A digital twin that models these assets without condition, performance, or energy data isn't predicting anything. It's a visualisation with a gap where intelligence should be. An automation layer that can't see the majority of the rotating fleet isn't autonomous. It has a blind spot the size of your most critical assets.

The monitoring gap isn't just a maintenance problem. It's the ceiling on every digitalisation programme that depends on asset-level data.

One layer. Every motor. No sensors on the asset.

The visibility layer reads the electrical signature of any AC motor from the motor control cabinet. Every motor draws current from its supply. When mechanical conditions change, the electrical signature changes in specific, measurable ways.

Bearing wear shifts harmonic patterns. Cavitation alters load signatures. Misalignment creates characteristic sideband frequencies. The visibility layer captures these changes continuously, from the MCC, without touching the asset.

Remote

Monitors from the motor control cabinet, not the asset. No sensors in hazardous zones. No cabling to submerged equipment. No access permits required.

Continuous

Samples the full electrical waveform, 24/7. Not route-based. Not periodic. Every operating hour generates data.

Scalable

One cabinet installation covers every motor connected to it. A site with 200 motors doesn't need 200 sensor installations. It needs access to the MCCs.

How does it work? → See the platform →

Condition, performance, and energy data from every connected motor.

Condition: developing faults detected early.

Mechanical: bearing degradation, imbalance, misalignment, coupling wear, looseness. Detected through changes in rotational harmonics and characteristic fault frequencies in the current spectrum.

Hydraulic: cavitation, blockage, dry running, seal degradation. Detected through load pattern analysis and process-related frequency shifts.

Electrical: insulation breakdown, winding faults, rotor bar defects, supply imbalance. Detected through voltage and current signature deviations.

Performance: operating efficiency tracked continuously.

Load profiling, operating regime classification, and deviation from baseline performance. A pump drawing 15% more power than its baseline is degrading, undersized, or running against a blocked discharge. The visibility layer flags it before maintenance sees a work order.

Energy: consumption measured per asset, per hour.

Continuous power consumption at asset level. Not estimated from nameplate data. Measured from the actual electrical signature. This is the data sustainability teams need for ESG reporting, energy audits, and efficiency improvement programmes. Vitens achieved a 7.1% efficiency gain across monitored pump stations using this data.

ESA does not replace vibration monitoring on assets that already have it. It covers the assets vibration cannot reach. On shared assets, ESA often detects faults vibration misses. At Schiphol Airport, SAM4 identified bearing damage in a gearbox motor that vibration monitoring had not detected.

For DOL motors in brownfield plants, ESA is not supplementary monitoring. It is the only source of condition, performance, and energy data these assets have ever had.

ESA vs vibration: what each method covers →

Named customers. Quantified outcomes. Five industries.

Water & Wastewater

Yorkshire Water

1,000+ sewage pumping stations monitored. 70,000 annual inspection trips replaced. Three pollution events prevented from a single detection. Sole supplier of ESA technology.

Chemicals

DuPont

40% reduction in equipment downtime across three European sites. 15+ failures detected. €1.7M in costs prevented over three years.

Aviation

Schiphol Airport

9 out of 9 reported failures detected. Bearing damage in a gearbox motor caught by SAM4, missed by vibration. More than 5 hours of downtime avoided.

Metals

ArcelorMittal

Bearing defect in a blast furnace cooling pump detected 5 months before failure. €50K+ in unplanned downtime avoided.

Metals

Nyrstar

"The invisible pain points become visible." ROI within months of deployment.

See all customer results → Start your asset audit

The visibility layer doesn't just generate data. It's built to act on it.

As the condition dataset grows across industries, asset types, and failure modes, the layer evolves from monitoring to autonomous decision support, closing the loop between detection and action.

See what your assets are telling you.

Every AC motor already carries a signal. The visibility layer reads it.
